Off Calendar Nov-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF ILYA SARKISKYAN'S MOTION FOR ORDER IMPOSING TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT BAMX USA MOTORCYCLES, INC., FOR FAILURE TO OBEY ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSE TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY.

APPEARANCES: Michael Martinovsky, Esq. of Law Office of Michael Martinovsky 230-5360 for pltf & moving party; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANT, COURT STRIKES THE ANSWER OF DEFENDANT BAMX USA MOTORCYCLES, INC. AND ENTERS DEFAULT AGAINST DEFENDANT BAMX US MOTORCYCLES, INC. PLAINTIFF TO OBTAIN $2,245.00 IN ADDITIONAL MONETARY SANCTIONS, PAYABLE WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. In addition, the Court clarified that the sanctions are against BAMX only. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-16-2010 Continued To Feb-25-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Moition For Order Comelling Answers To Written Discovery (Ccp 2030.290(B)) And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Daniel Tabakh, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: ILYA SARKISKYAN

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF's Motion To Compel Answers To Written Discovery is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant BAMX USA Motorsports, Inc.'s failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. BAMX USA Motorsports, Inc. is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant BAMX USA Motorsports, Inc. in the amount of $565, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-20-2010 Continued To Aug-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Re-Ntc Of Mtn For Appointment Of Receiver
Off Calendar Apr-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ILYA SARKISYAN Re-Notice of Motion for Appointment of Receiver. Appearances: Michael Martinovsky, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 230-5360 appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party; Joseph Wood, Esq. of Hennefer Finley & Wood LLP (415) 421-6100 speaking for all defendants at this motion. Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion stayed, and off calendar, as to Defendants named in the Notice of Bankruptcy and automatic stay filed April 5, 2010. Defendant BAMX USA, Motorcycles, Inc. only is not stayed. The Court grants Plaintiff motion to appoint receiver as to Defendant BAMX USA, Motorcycles, Inc. The Court cites CCP section 564(b)(1), Plaintiff's property or fund is in danger of being lost, removed, or materially injured. The Court appoints Susan L. Uecker as receiver for Defendant BAMX USA, Motorcycles, Inc. Ms. Uecker to post Bond in the amount of $5,000.00. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
